mechanism_narrative: A persistent downward trend in FEDFUNDS over the trailing 3 months lowers the discount rate applied to equity cash flows and signals easier financial conditions, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days regardless of macro regime.
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS 3-month trend and 20d forward returns stays below 0.03 over any rolling 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE rises more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months, it crosses the Sahm-rule-adjacent threshold signaling labor market deterioration that triggers rapid negative repricing of equities within the next 5 trading days as investors anticipate recession.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns are positive or flat (mean > 0) in more than 55% of triggered instances across the sample period.

mechanism_narrative: Rising RSAFS combined with falling FEDFUNDS jointly signal resilient consumer demand under easing monetary conditions, a combination that supports sustained equity valuation expansion over a full quarter; neither series alone captures this reflexive demand-liquidity interaction.
regime_hint: monetary easing cycle with resilient consumer
failure_condition: Interaction term coefficient (RSAFS growth x FEDFUNDS change) is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in walk-forward regression over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion regime, decelerating INDPRO growth signals waning industrial momentum and margin compression risk, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days; this effect is muted or absent during early-cycle recoveries when slack absorption dominates.
failure_condition: Directional accuracy of INDPRO deceleration signal for 20d returns falls below 50% in a rolling 3-year backtest confined to late-cycle periods.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in PAYEMS exceeding 100,000 jobs signals an abrupt labor demand shock that triggers immediate risk-off equity flows, producing negative S&P 500 returns within the following 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Mean 5-day forward return following triggered PAYEMS declines is not significantly different from unconditional mean (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sustained uptrend in HOUST over 6 months reflects expanding housing-driven demand and consumer confidence, which feeds through to broader equity earnings expectations and produces positive S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: Rank IC between 6-month HOUST trend and 60d forward returns falls below 0.03 over any rolling 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL year-over-year inflation exceeds 4% while FEDFUNDS remains below the level implied by a Taylor-rule response, markets price in future aggressive tightening, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as real rate expectations rise sharply.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL YoY > 4% and FEDFUNDS below trailing 12-month average
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with inflation overshoot
failure_condition: SPX 20d forward returns are positive in more than 55% of triggered instances, or joint trigger occurs fewer than 5 times in backtest making inference unreliable.

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary tightening regime (FEDFUNDS rising over trailing 6 months), a simultaneous rise in UNRATE signals that tightening has begun damaging labor markets, prompting anticipatory equity selloffs over the next 20 trading days that do not occur when UNRATE rises during an easing regime.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS rising over trailing 6 months and UNRATE rises > 0.1pp
failure_condition: Directional accuracy of the joint UNRATE-FEDFUNDS signal for 20d returns during tightening regimes does not exceed 50% over any rolling 3-year window.
